detail: Google raised 2026 CapEx guidance from $175-185B to $180-190B, exceeding 100% YoY growth. Investment focuses on high-performance GPU clusters, in-house ASIC (TPU) development, and next-gen data centers for high-power-density computing.

HBM4 will stick with microbumps (10µm pitch) instead of hybrid bonding. JEDEC revised height limit from 720µm to 775µm. Custom base dies expected from AMD and Nvidia. HBM4E expected 2027 with hybrid bonding potentially for 18-20 high stacks.

HBM failures are the number one cause of GPU/accelerator failures in data centers. HBM3 to HBM4 transition requires 2,048-bit interface with significantly more TSVs. 16-high stacks with shrinking bump pitch. Testing shifting left with wafer-level burn-in critical.

TPU (Tensor Processing Unit) excels at parallel matrix multiplications and tensor operations, the core mathematical operations in neural networks. AI accelerators prioritize high-bandwidth transfers into and out of memory, resulting in faster computations and less electricity use vs GPUs.

AI accelerator modules are 100mm x 100mm today, moving to 150mm x 150mm. Testing requires system-level approach. UCIe interfaces used for die-to-die communication. CoWoS packaging critical for multi-die integration.

Google announced TPU v6e (Trillium) at Cloud Next 2024. Trillium offers 4.7x improvement in compute performance per chip vs TPU v5e. Features 2x HBM capacity and 2x interchip interconnect bandwidth. Generally available in December 2024.

Google announced TPU v7 (Ironwood) at Google I/O 2025. Ironwood is designed for inference workloads, delivering 10x compute improvement over TPU v6e. Features 192GB HBM per chip and new SparseCore architecture. Optimized for large language model inference.

TPU v5p became generally available in 2024. Each TPU v5p chip delivers 2x FLOPS and 3x memory bandwidth vs TPU v4. Pods can scale to 8,960 chips with 4,800 Gbps ICI bandwidth per chip. Used for training Gemini models.

Google TPU manufactured by TSMC using advanced process nodes (5nm for v5p, 4nm for v6e). Uses TSMC CoWoS packaging for HBM integration. HBM supplied by SK Hynix (primary) and Samsung. ABF substrate from Ibiden and Shinko.

Google TPU reduces NVIDIA dependency for internal AI workloads. TPU used for Gemini, Search, YouTube, Cloud AI services. However, Google Cloud still offers NVIDIA H100/B200 instances. TPU advantages: cost efficiency for large-scale training/inference. NVIDIA advantages: broader ecosystem, CUDA dominance.

CSP self-developed chips trend: Google TPU (most mature), AWS Trainium/Inferentia, Microsoft Maia, Meta MTIA. Collectively reducing NVIDIA dependency but NVIDIA still dominates 80%+ AI accelerator market. Custom chips offer 30-50% cost advantage for specific workloads.

TPU v5p uses TSMC CoWoS-S packaging with 2.5D silicon interposer. HBM3 stacks (4-high) integrated via microbumps. ABF substrate with 14-18 layers. Package size approximately 75mm x 75mm. Power consumption ~400W per chip.
